name: Fergus
description_short: Fergus is job management software for tradespeople that helps quote, schedule, invoice, and get paid for jobs. It is not a financial product or lender.
description_long:
Fergus is a cloud-based job management platform designed specifically for tradespeople in industries including electrical, plumbing, HVAC, building, landscaping, painting, roofing, and repairs/maintenance. The company was founded to address the administrative burden that trades professionals face, allowing them to focus on billable work rather than paperwork and backend operations.

Fergus offers an all-in-one job management suite with features including professional quote generation, customizable scheduling and job assignment, digital job cards containing quotes, purchase orders, job costs and customer details, automatic invoice creation with cost matching, and integrated payment processing via Tap to Pay on iPhone and Android. The platform includes mobile apps for both iOS and Android, real-time tracking of time and materials, safety reporting, and integrations with 100+ suppliers and accounting tools including QuickBooks, Xero, and FreshBooks. Pricing is straightforward with an Essentials Plan starting at $31/month and a Pro Plan starting at $44/month.

Fergus distinguishes itself through transparent pricing with no job credits, caps, or hidden fees, a focus on reducing administrative time (marketing case studies show up to 1 day per month saved versus 1 day per week), and deep integrations with trade supplier systems for automatic invoice importing and job matching. The platform has built a user base of 20,000+ tradespeople with 290+ customer reviews and emphasizes ease of use and professional presentation for small trades businesses.

It is important to note that Fergus is software-as-a-service (SaaS) business management software, not a consumer finance product. While it includes a Tap to Pay payment processing feature called Fergus Pay, the company is fundamentally a workflow and accounting tool for contractors, not a lender, alternative financial services provider, or credit product. Its miscategorization as a payday-alternative is factually inaccurate.
services: Job scheduling with team assignment and calendar management
Digital job cards with quotes, purchase orders, costs, and customer details
Automatic invoice creation and cost matching
Tap to Pay mobile payment processing on iOS and Android
Time and materials tracking in real-time
Integration with 100+ suppliers for automatic invoice import
Accounting software integrations (QuickBooks, Xero, FreshBooks, Zapier)
Supplier pricebook uploads for accurate job pricing
Mobile apps for iOS and Android with offline functionality
Customer contact and job history management
pros: All-in-one platform eliminates need for multiple disconnected tools for quoting, scheduling, invoicing, and payment processing
Transparent, simple pricing with no hidden job credits, caps, or surprise add-ons that penalize growth
Integrates with 100+ suppliers and accounting platforms (QuickBooks, Xero, Zapier) to automate data entry and cost matching
Mobile-first design works on iOS and Android with offline functionality and real-time sync across team members
Tap to Pay feature enables faster cash flow and payment collection directly on the job site
Dedicated support team and 14-day free trial with no credit card required for onboarding
Customer testimonials show measurable business impact: up to 20-30% sales increase, reduced admin time, and improved cash flow visibility
cons: Limited to specific industries (trades) and not suitable for non-construction/service businesses
Pricing starts at $31/month but scales with team size and feature usage; total cost of ownership unclear for larger crews
No mention of data security standards, compliance certifications (SOC 2, GDPR), or uptime SLA guarantees on website
Tap to Pay payment processing feature availability and transaction fees not detailed; unclear if this is proprietary or white-labeled
Platform requires digital adoption from entire team; may face resistance from older tradespeople less comfortable with mobile software
best_for: Independent and small-crew tradespeople (electricians, plumbers, HVAC technicians, builders) looking to reduce administrative overhead
Trades businesses with 2-20 employees seeking professional quoting and invoicing without expensive enterprise software
Contractors who perform on-site work and need mobile access, scheduling, and on-the-job payment collection
Growing trades companies that want to improve job costing accuracy and profitability visibility
diagnosis: Fergus is software for tradespeople to manage their business operations, not a consumer financial product or lending service. It should not be categorized as a payday-alternative, emergency loan provider, or any consumer finance category. It belongs in business-loans or small-business-software and is best suited for independent contractors and small trades companies seeking to streamline quoting, scheduling, and invoicing workflows.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
